text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic myomectomy on a degenerated posterior wall lower segment uterine fibroid. The broad ligament peritoneum on the posterior aspect of the uterus is dissected away, and the fibroid is identified. Vasopressin is infiltrated into the myometrial wall adjacent to the fibroid. A cut is made over the myometrium using cold knife scissors. Due to the fibroid's degeneration, it is removed using traction and counter traction with needle holders.

The cut end of the uterine musculature shows minimal bleeding due to vasopressin infiltration. The surgeon performs laparoscopic suturing on the posterior lower end of the fibroid using figure-of-eight sutures with vicryl, navigating the challenging needle angulation due to the fibroid's large sub-serosal component.

The surgeon uses a T-lift device to suspend the ovaries, particularly on the left side, preventing them from interfering with the suturing path and field of vision. A three-dimensional laparoscope provides enhanced depth perception, aiding in suturing the low-lying posterior fibroid in the pelvis.
The surgeon uses a MorSafe bag to morcellate and extract the myoma from the abdominal cavity, ensuring safe removal of the fibroid tissue.
